key_insights.insight: The key to dealing with anxiety is not to fight it, but to approach it with kindness and understanding, like a frightened animal.
key_insights.quote: They're attacking the part of themselves that's anxious and it makes it more anxious.

key_insights.insight: Shifting from the left hemisphere (anxiety, control) to the right hemisphere (creativity, curiosity) can toggle off anxiety.
key_insights.quote: Just as anxiety shuts off creativity, creativity can shut down anxiety. It's likethese two parts of the brain toggle.

key_insights.insight: Our modern culture, dominated by the left hemisphere, has alienated us from our inherent sense of meaning and purpose.
key_insights.quote: We live in a bizarre, crazy culture, and we do not know why we're here because we don'thave access to our sense of meaning.

key_insights.insight: A key element of freedom and meaning is to follow your own truth, even if it leads you to a counter-cultural life.
key_insights.quote: If you serve your true nature, there will come a time when you become countercultural.You do something that is not what your parents approved of,or it's not what your religion taught.

key_insights.insight: Our bodies are honest; it is only the verbal mind that can lie, and when we lie, our bodies reflect that by becoming very weak.
key_insights.quote: The body lives in reality. The body is honest.Only the mind and only the verbal mind can lie to usand tell us things that we can believeeven though they're not true.

key_insights.insight: You find your purpose by finding where your deep gladness and the world's deep hunger meet, which is fundamentally about what happens between people, not to them.
key_insights.quote: The meaning of life is not what happens to people. The meaning oflife, your purpose in life, is what happens between people.

key_insights.insight: A great story is defined when bad things happen to heroes, and do they allow suffering to consume them, or do they make something wonderful?
key_insights.quote: A good story is when bad things happen to good people, but a great story is when bad things happen to heroes.

actionable_advice.advice: Calm a frightened part of yourself by giving it space and telling it you are there to listen and provide support.
actionable_advice.how_to: Find the anxious part of yourself, acknowledge the anxiety, give it space, and listen to what it has to say without judgement.

actionable_advice.advice: Use sensory imagination, such as vividly imagining eating an orange, to shift brain activity from the left to the right hemisphere and alleviate anxiety.
actionable_advice.how_to: Engage all your senses to vividly imagine an experience that brings relaxation and joy.

actionable_advice.advice: If you don't want to do something, and you don't have to do something, don't do it.
actionable_advice.how_to: Be honest with yourself about what you truly want and need and stop saying yes to things that drain you or go against your true self.
When experiencing suffering, offer yourself loving kindness by saying "May I be well, may I be happy, may I be free from suffering, may I feel safe and protected, may I live with ease."

memorable_stories: Martha describes her near-death experience during surgery and how it changed her life.
Martha tells the story of how her mother admitted to knowing about her father's abuse, then retracted it.
Martha shares her experiences of seeing people care for her sick daughter in a South African airport, contrasting this with fearful stereotypes of Africa.
Martha explains how she and her then-husband both came out as gay, and the fire hose of love came when her partner also fell in love with a female guest.
contrarian_claims: Anxiety isn't a broken machine, it's a frightened animal and should be approached with gentleness, not violence.
Productivity based on being 'terrified' is detrimental, not beneficial.
The most helpful and best response to a violent terrorist is to gently ask about and empathize with their experience.
Leaving Mormonism was her only option.
A great story is only defined when it happens to heroes.
The best way to make decisions is based on how free it feels.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Martha Beck (Oprah's Life Coach): I Nearly Died, So I Stopped Lying! Why You're Anxious & How To Fix It! Fix Your Childhood Trauma!
